events.title: 1:1 Tech Mentor Appointment
events.description:
Free.  By appointment only.        Call for availability.  Same day booking not always possible.  Offered in  English, Español,  or  Tiếng Việt.   Would you like to find out more about computers? Learn at your own pace!   What We Do:   
  Intro to computer basics  Account or device setup  Intro to Microsoft Office  Job search & resume help  Navigating/organizing email  And more!  
 Contact Seven Trees by phone  (408) 808-3056  or email at   tiffany.lam@sjl

events.title: Love Letters to the Library: Create Your Own Coloring Valentine!
events.location: Bascom — Children's Area (near the children's fiction section)

events.title: Paper Chains of Kindness
events.description:
Join us for a creative and heartwarming art activity where every link counts! Write a message of kindness, gratitude, or encouragement on a colorful strip of paper, then add it to our growing community chain. Together, we’ll build a beautiful visual reminder of how small acts of kindness connect us all. All ages welcome—come share your creativity and help our chain grow! 
 Free. No registration is required. 
 
  This program is developed to align with  the Early Education Quality Standards . Thi

events.title: Planning & Transportation Commission Meeting
events.description: Tune in to the Planning and Transportation Commission Meeting held in Council Chambers and virtually via Zoom.

events.title: Read-A-Thon (Partnership with Cambrian Educational Foundation)
events.description:
Cambrian School District Students:  pick up or download your reading materials from February 1 through February 28 for the District-wide Read-A-Thon, which is being run by our Partner, Cambrian Educational Foundation. 
 The student who reads the most in their grade and school (Bagby Elementary, Fammatre Elementary, Farnham Charter Elementary, Sartorette Charter Elementary, Steindorf STEAM, and Price Charter Middle School) will win a special prize for reading excellence from the Ca

events.title: Baby Storytime
events.description:
A musical storytime for infants, babies, and new toddlers with books, baby bounces, and dancing! After storytime, we will host a brief Stay & Play for grownups to connect and mingle. This program is designed for ages 0-18 months.

Assisted Listening Devices available upon request.

events.title: (Virtual) Silicon Valley Reads Book Discussion: Iona Iverson's Rules for Commuting
events.description: Join us to discuss the novel  "Iona Iverson's Rules for Commuting" by Clare Pooley , a heartwarming story that proves the most rigid social contract—never talk to strangers—is meant to be broken.    Iona Iverson, an eccentric advice columnist, lives by an ironclad rule: ignore everyone on her daily train ride, whom she knows only by self-assigned nicknames like "Smart-But-Sexist-Manspreader." But when a fellow commuter suddenly chokes, a single, life-saving act of interventio

events.title: SPECIAL Silicon Valley Reads Edition:  (Virtual) Cambrian and Pearl Mystery Book Club
events.description:
This month we will be discussing    Iona Iverson's Rules for Commuting  by  Clare Pooley      Iona Iverson lives by an ironclad rule: ignore everyone on her daily train ride. But when a fellow commuter suddenly chokes, a single, life-saving act of intervention shatters Iona's carefully constructed isolation & transforms a train car of strangers into a true community.   Registration is required to receive a link and password. Deadline to sign-up will be 1 hour before the program begins.

events.title: Reading on the Avenue Book Club
events.description:
The Reading on the Avenue Book Club meets on the fourth Wednesday of the month at 7 PM.  
 Newcomers welcome!   2026 Book Selections:   
   January (non-fiction) :   A Woman of No Importance   : The Untold Story of an American Spy Who Helped Win WWII   by Sonia Purnell    February (fiction):    Yellowface   by R. F. Kuang     March (classic fiction) :  The Scapegoat  by Daphne Du Maurier  (title available through LINK+)    April (non-fiction):    Who Is Government?   &nb

events.title: 3D Printing On Demand
events.location: Vineland — Correspondence online and pickup in person
events.description:
Want to get something printed on our 3D printer? 
 Participants can choose a design from  Thingiverse , or provide their own design. Designs must be in an .stl file format and able to be emailed. We estimate that 3D prints will be ready for pickup within 7 days of submission. Designs are considered printable if able to load and save on a  Creality K1C , but the program lead has final say. All prints must take less than 1 hr 30 min (estimated 60 grams of filament). All prints are free and must me

events.title: City/School Liaison Committee Meeting
events.description: Watch and participate in the City/School Liaison Committee Meeting as the committee discusses topics related to our Palo Alto schools.

events.description: The City/School Transportation Safety Committee is composed of Palo Alto Council of PTAs appointed Parent Transportation Safety Representatives, City of Palo Alto staff and Palo Alto Unified School District staff.

events.description: Join us for a screening of How to Build a Library, a documentary about two Nairobi women who transformed a colonial-era library into a vibrant cultural hub.

events.title: Drop-in Chess Play

events.description: Whether you're a grandmaster or just learning the basics, our friendly and welcoming environment is perfect for all levels. Drop in to play casual games, learn new strategies, or simply enjoy the company of fellow chess enthusiasts.

events.title: eBook Help: For Seniors
events.description:
Do you need help downloading an ebook, or accessing digital content?  Get hands-on help with borrowing and downloading FREE library eBooks and eAudiobooks to your Kindle, Nook, iPad or smartphone. You are welcome to bring your own device to each session. We can also help you search for and use other awesome library eResources, like one of our many article databases or online tutorials to learn something new.

events.title: Income Tax Preparation by Tax-Aid
events.description:
Tax-Aid  will provide FREE basic 2025 income tax return preparation to qualified individuals who earned $67,000 or less on   Saturdays, February 7 and March 14 and 28.      
  This service is drop-in, first come, first served!  Please arrive as early as possible to sign up.  The last file will be accepted no later than 2 PM!      
Items to bring with you:     
 (Please bring copies of your tax documents) 
  Face masks are recommended!   Photo ID  Social

events.title: Storytime Around the World
events.description: A special storytime series that celebrates a different culture from across the globe each session! The storytime will highlight unique traditions, customs, and communities, inviting children to explore the world through storytelling. Perfect for curious minds and young explorers, this program offers a fun and thoughtful way to learn about diverse cultures, with a few new words and lots of new perspectives.

events.title: Finding Marian Abel at Los Altos  Library
events.description: Join longtime library commissioner Pierre Bedard and LAHS' Gustav Herhne for a special book club celebrating Marian Abel’s History of the Los Altos Library (1914-1964). The history, written in 1972, explores the library’s first 50 years. Register and get a PDF of her work so you can prepare for the book club. Marian Abel loved Los Altos. Let's celebrate our history and look forward to the next fifty years!    For accessibility accommodations (ex. ASL), please fill out the online form at sccld.or

events.title: Adult Book Discussion: Yellow Face by R.F. Kuang
events.description:
In March, the Cupertino Book Discussion Group will talk about R.F. Kuang's novel  Yellowface, (2023) A satirical but sharp critique of both the publishing industry and the racism of both publishers and the reading public.  
 Struggling novelist June Hayward is celebrating her fellow Yale alumna Athena Liu's lucrative new movie deal  when Athena chokes to death in a freak accident. June, desperately jealous of Athena's tremendous literary success, steals the mos

events.title: Amplify: Japanese Calligraphy
events.description:
Discover the beauty and tradition of Japanese calligraphy in this hands-on program. Explore the history and cultural significance of calligraphy, learn how to make traditional sumi ink, and practice essential brush techniques through guided instruction and creative practice.

Ages 12 and up. 

This event is part of Amplify: Teen Voices, a program for teens who want to share their interests and passions with an audience.

events.title: External Event: Preserving Natural Darkness
events.description: Canyon Conversations: Preserving Natural Darkness     Join McDonald Observatory and Grand Canyon Conservancy for a free, expert-led virtual panel discussion exploring the urgent need to preserve natural night skies.   Grand Canyon National Park enjoys some of the darkest night skies in the continental United States. events.title: Mountain View Patents
events.description:
In partnership with the Mountain View Historical Association, this hands-on STEAM program introduces kids to real patents created in Mountain View. Participants will rotate through stations to explore local patents, learn how patents work, and then design their own invention by creating a patent of their own.

 





 



 

This event is generously sponsored by the Friends of the Mountain View Library.

events.description:
Starting Time: 2:30 pm Ending Time: 4:00 pm   Community Room 201A   On the first Friday of each month we will meet in Room 201B  
      Cupertino Library Knit Alongs continue in 2026!.  All adult knitters and crocheters are welcome. No registration or sign up required.  We're always happy to meet someone new, so please feel free to join us.  Meet new friends, start a new project, or finish an old one.    *Please note, This is mostly a social group for

events.title: Amplify: Research Symposium
events.description: Monta Vista High School's Interdisciplinary Research Project Design class presents a showcase of student-led projects. From environmental research to mental health initiatives, these projects highlight students’ creativity and commitment to making a positive impact locally and globally. Learn about the innovative ideas young researchers have turned into meaningful change.
